meta.title: TGD Pakistan militant network pack
meta.scope: Pakistan-relevant militant organisations, named factions, directly linked fronts, and key individuals with public-source relationship evidence.
meta.selection_note: This is a curated research set, not a reproduction of every name on a government list. A Pakistan proscription, UN listing, current activity assessment, and TGD analytical category are separate fields and should not be treated as interchangeable.

nacta-2024.label: Pakistan NACTA: Proscribed Organisations List, updated 6 October 2024

un-1267.label: UN Security Council ISIL (Da'esh) and Al-Qaida Sanctions List

un-xml.label: UN Security Council sanctions data, XML edition

state-bla-2025.label: U.S. State Department: Terrorist designation of the BLA and Majeed Brigade, 11 August 2025

nacta-watch-2020.label: Pakistan NACTA Watch 2020-21: BRAS composition and Baloch militant networks

nctc-ttp.label: U.S. National Counterterrorism Center: TTP profile

nctc-let.label: U.S. National Counterterrorism Center: Lashkar-e-Tayyiba profile

nctc-jem.label: U.S. National Counterterrorism Center: Jaish-e-Mohammed profile

designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.132

nodes.label: Noor Wali Mehsud
nodes.summary: UN-listed leader of Tehrik-e Taliban Pakistan following the death of Maulana Fazlullah.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.427

nodes.label: Maulana Fazlullah
nodes.summary: Former TTP leader and earlier commander of the movement in Swat. The UN record confirms his death on 13 June 2018.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.352

nodes.label: Jamaat-ul-Ahrar
nodes.summary: UN-listed TTP splinter formed in Mohmand in 2014 and documented in the Pakistan-Afghanistan border region.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.152
United States: Specially Designated Global Terrorist organisation

nodes.label: Hizb-ul-Ahrar
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for a faction that emerged from the Jamaat-ul-Ahrar milieu.

nodes.label: Tariq Gidar Group
nodes.summary: UN-listed TTP splinter formed in Darra Adam Khel in 2007.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.160

nodes.label: TTP Swat
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Tehrik-e-Taliban Swat.

nodes.label: TTP Mohmand
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Tehrik-e-Taliban Mohmand.

nodes.label: TTP Bajaur
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Tehrik-e-Taliban Bajaur.

nodes.label: Hafiz Gul Bahadur Group
nodes.summary: Pakistan proscribed the Hafiz Gul Bahadur Group in July 2024. TGD tracks it separately from TTP while preserving documented ecosystem links.

nodes.label: Lashkar-e-Islam
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Lashkar-e-Islam, a network historically associated with the Khyber theatre.

nodes.label: TNSM
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Tehrik-e-Nifaz-e-Shariat-e-Mohammadi, historically associated with Malakand and Swat.

nodes.label: Ghazi Force
nodes.summary: Organisation listed by Pakistan under the Anti-Terrorism Act. Current operational status requires continuing review.

nodes.label: Abdullah Azzam Brigade
nodes.summary: Organisation listed by Pakistan in March 2013. The name can refer to different networks in different theatres, so this node is limited to the Pakistan designation record.

nodes.label: 313 Brigade
nodes.summary: Organisation listed by Pakistan in March 2013. Relationship claims beyond the designation record remain subject to source review.

designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.004
UN ISIL and Al-Qaida sanctions regime
UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.161
United States: Foreign Terrorist Organization branch

nodes.label: Maulawi Rajab
nodes.summary: UN-listed senior leader of Islamic State Khorasan Province.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.434

nodes.label: Sultan Aziz Azam
nodes.summary: UN-listed spokesperson of Islamic State Khorasan Province.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.435

nodes.label: Lashkar-e-Tayyiba
nodes.summary: Pakistan-based organisation listed by Pakistan and the UN. The UN treats Jamaat-ud-Dawa as an alias and the U.S. designates LeT as an FTO.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.118

nodes.label: Jamaat-ud-Dawa
nodes.summary: Pakistan lists Jamaat-ud-Dawa separately; the UN sanctions record identifies it as an alias of Lashkar-e-Tayyiba.
designations: UN: listed as an alias of Lashkar-e-Tayyiba

nodes.label: Falah-e-Insaniat Foundation
nodes.summary: Pakistan proscription record for a charity documented by U.S. government sources as affiliated with the LeT/JuD network.

designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.263

nodes.label: Zaki-ur-Rehman Lakhvi
nodes.summary: UN sanctions record identifies Lakhvi as chief of operations of Lashkar-e-Tayyiba.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.264

nodes.label: Haji Muhammad Ashraf
nodes.summary: UN sanctions record identifies Ashraf as chief of finance of Lashkar-e-Tayyiba.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.265

nodes.label: Zafar Iqbal
nodes.summary: UN sanctions record describes Iqbal as a co-founder and senior leader who held finance, education, and medical-wing roles in LeT/JuD.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.308

nodes.label: Sajid Mir
nodes.summary: Rewards for Justice identifies Mir as a senior LeT member wanted for involvement in the November 2008 Mumbai attacks.

nodes.label: Abdul Rehman Makki
nodes.role: LeT deputy leader and political-affairs figure
nodes.summary: UN sanctions record identifies Makki as deputy amir of LeT and head of political affairs in the JuD/LeT network.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.433

nodes.label: Jaish-e-Mohammed
nodes.summary: Pakistan- and UN-listed organisation founded by Masood Azhar. The UN record documents relationships with HuM, LeT, Al-Akhtar Trust, and HuJI.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.019
UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDi.422

nodes.label: Khuddam-ul-Islam
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Khuddam-ul-Islam. NCTC reporting described it as one of the names emerging from JeM's 2003 split.

nodes.label: Jamat-ul-Furqan
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Jamat-ul-Furqan. NCTC reporting described it as one of the factions emerging from JeM's 2003 split.

nodes.label: Harakat ul-Mujahidin
nodes.summary: UN-listed organisation documented as associated with JeM, LeJ, and LeT and active in Pakistan and Afghanistan.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.008

nodes.label: Harakat-ul Jihad Islami
nodes.summary: UN-listed organisation associated with JeM and HuM in the sanctions record.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.130

nodes.label: Al-Akhtar Trust International
nodes.summary: UN record says the trust was registered by JeM members and associated with HuM, LeJ, and LeT.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.121
Pakistan: enlisted under UN Security Council Resolution 1267

nodes.label: Al Rashid Trust
nodes.summary: UN record describes the trust as reportedly defunct, formerly headquartered in Pakistan, involved in financing al-Qaeda and the Taliban, and associated with JeM.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.005
Pakistan: enlisted under UN Security Council Resolution 1267

nodes.label: Lashkar-e-Jhangvi
nodes.summary: Pakistan- and UN-listed organisation. The UN record places its historic base primarily in Punjab and Karachi and documents links to other Pakistan-based networks.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.096

nodes.label: Sipah-e-Sahaba Pakistan
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Sipah-e-Sahaba Pakistan and its later listed names Millat-e-Islamia Pakistan and Ahle Sunnat Wal Jamaat.

nodes.label: Lashkar-e-Jhangvi Al-Alami
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for the Al-Alami faction of Lashkar-e-Jhangvi.

nodes.label: Sipah-i-Muhammad Pakistan
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Sipah-i-Muhammad Pakistan.

designations: United States: Foreign Terrorist Organization, designation updated 11 August 2025

nodes.label: Majeed Brigade
nodes.role: BLA unit / alias in U.S. designation
nodes.summary: Pakistan lists the Majeed Brigade separately. In August 2025, the U.S. State Department added it as an alias of the BLA in the BLA's FTO designation.
designations: United States: BLA alias in FTO designation, 11 August 2025

nodes.label: Balochistan Liberation Front
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for the Balochistan Liberation Front, a major organisation in the Baloch separatist militant ecosystem.

nodes.label: Balochistan Republican Army
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for the Balochistan Republican Army.

nodes.label: United Baloch Army
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for the United Baloch Army.

nodes.label: Baloch Raaji Aajoi Sangar
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Baloch Raaji Aajoi Sangar, commonly abbreviated BRAS.

nodes.label: Balochistan Liberation United Front
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for the Balochistan Liberation United Front.

nodes.label: Balochistan National Liberation Army
nodes.summary: Organisation listed by Pakistan in August 2012. This node is kept separate from the BLA because the official list names it separately.

nodes.label: Sindhudesh Revolutionary Army
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for the Sindhudesh Revolutionary Army.

nodes.label: Sindhudesh Liberation Army
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for the Sindhudesh Liberation Army.

nodes.label: Jeay Sindh Muttahida Mahaz
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for Jeay Sindh Muttahida Mahaz. The node records legal status without treating proscription alone as proof of current armed activity.

nodes.label: Eastern Turkistan Islamic Movement
nodes.summary: UN-listed organisation with historic Afghanistan-Pakistan links; Pakistan lists ETIM as proscribed.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.088

nodes.label: Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan
nodes.summary: UN-listed organisation documented in the Afghanistan-Pakistan border region and associated with ETIM and the Islamic Jihad Group.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.010

nodes.label: Islamic Jihad Union
nodes.summary: Pakistan designation record for IJU; the UN lists the Islamic Jihad Group and documents its relationship with IMU.
designations: UN ISIL and Al-Qaida list: QDe.119

nodes.label: Jundullah
nodes.summary: Pakistan proscription record for Jundullah. Because several unrelated organisations have used this name, TGD does not merge this node with namesakes in Iran or other theatres without additional evidence.

nodes.label: Zainabiyoun Brigade
nodes.summary: Pakistan proscribed the Zainabiyoun Brigade in March 2024. It is tracked separately from Pakistan-domestic militant networks because its principal theatre has been Syria.
